Guide.Description: The model is a Structure-based model that gives a classification based on the mechanism of action of the substance to help in the assessment of the acute toxicity towards fish.
Guide.Model: The original model implements the conceptual scheme developed by Verhaar et al. For the implementation in ToxTree: Nina jeliazkova; IDEA Consult, Joseph II straat 40 B1, 1000 Brussels, Belgium; jeliazkova.nina@gmail.com
Guide.Dataset: Endpoint units: Adimensional. Dependent variable: The dependent variable is the mechanism of action organized in four classes: Class 1 (narcosis or baseline toxicity) Class 2 (less inert compounds) Class 3 (unspecific reactivity) Class 4 (compounds and groups of compounds acting by a specific mechanism) Class 5 (Not possible to classify according to these rules) Data for the dependent variable for the external validation set: All
Guide.Descriptors: The Verhaar classification is based on the same scheme as implemented in ToxTree, which is an open-source application. The scheme is expert-based and the model is structure-based. Ad hoc decision tree has been implemented within Toxtree and the same tree is implemented in VEGA. The model is a structure-based model and does not make use of descriptors.

xml.Reference: The Toxtree model has been implemented in VEGA by Alberto Manganaro; IRCCS-Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri, Via La Masa 19, 20156 Milano, Italy; alberto.manganaro@marionegri.it in 2020. It has been described here: Verhaar, H.J.M., van Leeuwen, C.J., Hermens, J.L.M., 1992. Classifying environmental-pollutants. 1. 481 Structure-activity-relationships for prediction of aquatic toxicity. Chemosphere 25, 471-491. For a general description of the VEGA software see: Benfenati E, Manganaro A, Gini G VEGA-QSAR: AI inside a platform for predictive toxicology Proceedings of the workshop "Popularize Artificial Intelligence 2013", December 5th 2013, Turin, Italy Published on CEUR Workshop Proceedings Vol-1107
xml.Endpoint: This model identifies the mechanism of action of substances for fish acute toxicity. The species is referred to fish acute toxicity. In particular, the model gives a classification based on the mechanism of action of the substance to help in the assessment of the acute toxicity towards fish.
